context.pr_title: fix #2331: solve problems in sensevoice_bin.py related to argmax and unique operations.
context.pr_body:
如issue #2331 提及，修复两个问题：
1. yseq = x.argmax(dim=-1) 改为 yseq = np.argmax(x, axis=-1)：

    - 由于 np.argmax 没有 dim 参数，原代码会导致 TypeError。改为 np.argmax(x, axis=-1) 以正确计算最大值的索引。    

3. 原来yseq = np.unique(yseq) 跟torch.unique_consecutive不一致（torch.unique_consecutive返回的结果是正确的），查了下：torch.unique_consecutive 只去除连续重复的元素，而np.unique：去除所有重复的元素，并返回排序后的唯一值数组。所以需要改为：

> mask = np.concatenate(([True], np.diff(yseq) != 0))
yseq = yseq[mask]

说明如下：
- np.diff(yseq) 计算数组中相邻元素的差值。
- np.diff(yseq) != 0 生成一个布尔数组，表示哪些位置的元素与前一个元素不同。
- np.concatenate(([True], ...)) 在布尔数组前添加一个 True，以保留第一个元素。
- yseq[mask] 使用布尔掩码提取去重后的数组。

经过测试后，结果符合预期。

agents.name: analysis_agent
agents.system_prompt:
You are an Analysis Agent specialized in Pull Request analysis. ## Project Context (MASCA Analysis) ## Project Analysis ### README [//]: # (
) ([简体中文](./README_zh.md)|English) [//]: # (# FunASR: A Fundamental End-to-End Speech Recognition Toolkit) [![SVG Banners](https://svg-banners.vercel.app/api?type=origin&text1=FunASR🤠&text2=💖%20A%20Fundamental%20End-to-End%20Speech%20Recognition%20Toolkit&width=800&height=210)](https://github.com/Akshay090/svg-banners) [![PyPI](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/funasr)](https://pypi.org/project/funasr/)

FunASR hopes to build a bridge between academic research and industrial applications on speech recognition. By supporting the training & finetuning of the industrial-grade speech recognition model, researchers and developers can conduct research and production of speech recognition models more conveniently, and promote the development of speech recognition ecology. ASR for Fun！ [**Highlights**](#highlights) | [**News**](https://github.com/alibaba-damo-academy/FunASR#whats-new) | [**Installation**](#installation) | [**Quick Start**](#quick-start) | [**Tutorial**](https://github.com/alibaba-damo-academy/FunASR/blob/main/docs/tutorial/README.md) | [**Runtime**](./runtime/readme.md) | [**Model Zoo**](#model-zoo) | [**Contact**](#contact) ## Highlights - FunASR is a fundamental speech recognition toolkit that offers a variety of features, including speech recognition (ASR), Voice Activity Detection (VAD), Punctuation Restoration, Language Models, Speaker Verification, Speaker Diarization and multi-talker ASR. FunASR provides convenient scripts and tutorials, supporting inference and fine-tuning of pre-trained models. - We have released a vast collection of academic and industrial pretrained models on the [ModelScope](https://www.modelscope.cn/models?page=1&tasks=auto-speech-recognition) and [huggingface](https://huggingface.co/FunASR), which can be accessed through our [Model Zoo](https://github.com/alibaba-damo-academy/FunASR/blob/main/docs/model_zoo/modelscope_models.md). The representative [Paraformer-large](https://www.modelscope.cn/models/damo/speech_paraformer-large_asr_nat-zh-cn-16k-common-vocab8404-pytorch/summary), a non-autoregressive end-to-end speech recognition model, has the advantages of high accuracy, high efficiency, and convenient deployment, supporting the rapid construction of speech recognition services. For more details on service deployment, please refer to the [service deployment document](runtime/readme_cn.md). ## What's new: - 2024/10/29: Real-time Transcription Service 1.12 released, The 2pass-offline mode supports the SensevoiceSmal model；([docs](runtime/readme.md)); - 2024/10/10：Added support for the W ## Your Task When you receive a PR title and body, you must: 1. **Analyze the request**: Understand what the PR wants to achieve 2. **Explore the code**: Use tools to navigate base_project and find relevant files/functions 3. **Identify targets**: Determine exactly which files and functions need to be modified 4. **Optimize MASCA**: Produce a version of the MASCA analysis focused on the specific PR ## Available Tools - `read_base_project_file(file_path)`: Read a file from the project - `list_base_project_directory(directory, pattern, recursive)`: List files/folders ## Required Output You must produce a structured output with: - `pr_title`: PR title - `pr_body`: PR body - `masca_optimized`: MASCA optimized for this specific PR - `files_to_modify`: List of files to modify with motivation - `functions_to_modify`: List of functions to modify with motivation - `analysis_summary`: Summary of your analysis ## Guidelines - Explore the code before drawing conclusions - Search for files with names relevant to the PR - Identify specific functions, not just generic files - Be precise with paths (relative to base_project) - For function names use the format "ClassName.method_name" for methods

Analyze this Pull Request:

## Title
fix #2331: solve problems in sensevoice_bin.py related to argmax and unique operations.

## Description
如issue #2331 提及，修复两个问题：
1. yseq = x.argmax(dim=-1) 改为 yseq = np.argmax(x, axis=-1)：

    - 由于 np.argmax 没有 dim 参数，原代码会导致 TypeError。改为 np.argmax(x, axis=-1) 以正确计算最大值的索引。    

3. 原来yseq = np.unique(yseq) 跟torch.unique_consecutive不一致（torch.unique_consecutive返回的结果是正确的），查了下：torch.unique_consecutive 只去除连续重复的元素，而np.unique：去除所有重复的元素，并返回排序后的唯一值数组。所以需要改为：

> mask = np.concatenate(([True], np.diff(yseq) != 0))
yseq = yseq[mask]

说明如下：
- np.diff(yseq) 计算数组中相邻元素的差值。
- np.diff(yseq) != 0 生成一个布尔数组，表示哪些位置的元素与前一个元素不同。
- np.concatenate(([True], ...)) 在布尔数组前添加一个 True，以保留第一个元素。
- yseq[mask] 使用布尔掩码提取去重后的数组。

经过测试后，结果符合预期。

Explore the source code to identify the files and functions to modify.

output.pr_title: fix #2331: solve problems in sensevoice_bin.py related to argmax and unique operations.
output.pr_body:
This PR fixes two issues in sensevoice_bin.py:
- Replace PyTorch argmax usage with NumPy argmax to avoid potential TypeError when dim parameter is not applicable. The change computes the index of the maximum logits per time step using np.argmax(x, axis=-1).
- Replace torch.unique_consecutive-based deduplication with a NumPy-based consecutive-duplicate removal approach. This aligns with torch.unique_consecutive behavior (removes only consecutive duplicates) by constructing a boolean mask that preserves the first element and drops consecutive duplicates, then applying the mask to obtain the deduplicated sequence. Finally, remove any blanks (self.blank_id) and decode tokens as usual.

Rationale:
- np.argmax(x, axis=-1) is the correct NumPy-equivalent for determining per-frame max indices and avoids TypeError from missing dim param.
- torch.unique_consecutive returns only consecutive duplicates, but the previous approach with np.unique removed all duplicates and sorted values, leading to mismatches. The proposed mask-based approach preserves the intended consecutive-duplicate removal behavior and supports proper decoding.

Impact:
- The sensevoice_bin inference path should now robustly handle argmax and consecutive-duplicate removal across CPU/GPU contexts, producing the same (or expected) token sequences as the torch-based approach intended by the original model behavior.

Tests:
- Validate by running inference on a small batch and ensuring decoded text matches expectations for representative inputs.
- Ensure behavior remains consistent for both CPU and GPU if applicable.
output.masca_optimized:
MASCA Optimized Analysis focused on PR #2331:

Targeted Change:
- runtime/python/libtorch/funasr_torch/sensevoice_bin.py -> SenseVoiceSmall.__call__
  - Modify per-frame argmax computation from PyTorch tensor-based to NumPy-based (np.argmax with axis) and remove usage of torch.unique_consecutive for deduplication. Implement a consecutive-duplicate removal via a NumPy mask to preserve the intended behavior.

Why this change is needed:
- The prior code used x.argmax(dim=-1) and torch.unique_consecutive, which can lead to type/method mismatches in some environments and does not align with the intended consecutive-duplicate removal semantics described in issue #2331.
- Using np.argmax(x, axis=-1) ensures a NumPy-compatible argmax when needed and avoids potential dim argument issues in certain contexts. The mask-based deduplication duplicates torch.unique_consecutive behavior by removing only consecutive duplicates, preventing unintended global deduplication and reordering.

Risks and mitigations:
- Risk: Data type/device (CPU/GPU) mismatches when converting between PyTorch tensors and NumPy arrays. Mitigation: detach/cpu() before .numpy() to operate on CPU NumPy arrays, then convert back as needed for downstream decoding (which happens on CPU by default in this path).
- Risk: Boundary cases with empty or very short sequences. Mitigation: mask and shape handling via NumPy operations ensures robust handling; token decoding remains gated by existing blank_id masking.

Testing guidance:
- Add/Run inference on a few audio samples to verify that the decoded token sequences match expected outcomes for representative cases (including cases with repeated consecutive logits and varying sequence lengths).
- Verify no regression for CPU/GPU workflows where ctc_logits could be numpy or torch tensor.

Expected outcome:
- Inference now uses a NumPy-based argmax and a consecutive-duplicate removal mask, preserving the intended behavior and improving compatibility across environments.

files_to_modify.reason: Replace PyTorch argmax usage and torch.unique_consecutive-based deduplication with a NumPy-based approach to compute per-frame max indices and remove consecutive duplicates, ensuring consistent decoding of tokens.

functions_to_modify.reason: Modify the per-batch decoding block: compute yseq via NumPy argmax and a mask-based consecutive-duplicate removal instead of x.argmax(dim=-1) followed by torch.unique_consecutive.

output.analysis_summary: Identified target for PR #2331: fix argmax usage and consecutive-duplicate removal in sensevoice_bin.py. The relevant code is in SenseVoiceSmall.__call__ inside runtime/python/libtorch/funasr_torch/sensevoice_bin.py. Proposed changes replace the PyTorch-based argmax and unique_consecutive flow with a NumPy-based approach: (1) compute yseq as np.argmax(x_np, axis=-1) after converting x to a NumPy array; (2) apply a mask to remove only consecutive duplicates using mask = np.concatenate(([True], np.diff(yseq) != 0)); (3) filter out blanks with a second mask and decode. This maintains the intended behavior (consecutive-duplicate removal) and avoids potential issues with torch.unique_consecutive and dim arguments. The plan minimizes risk by operating on CPU-side NumPy arrays, with careful handling of tensor-to-array conversion. The changes are localized to a single function, reducing blast radius and preserving overall architecture.

agents.name: context_planner_agent
agents.system_prompt:
You are a Context Planner Agent specialized in step plan generation.

## Your Task
You receive from the Analysis Agent:
- PR title and body
- Optimized MASCA
- List of files to modify
- List of functions to modify

You must:
1. **Retrieve context**: Use tools to read the context files of the identified functions
2. **Analyze dependencies**: Understand how the functions interact
3. **Generate step plan**: Create a detailed step-by-step plan

## Available Tools
- `read_context_file(file_path)`: Read a pre-generated context file
- `list_context_files(directory, pattern)`: List available context files
- `read_call_graph(section)`: Read information from the call graph

## Required Output
You must produce a `step_plan` with:
- `summary`: 1-2 sentence summary of what the PR does
- `steps`: Ordered list of steps, each with:
  - `operation`: What to do (e.g., "Add validation method")
  - `file_to_modify`: Relative path of the file to modify (e.g., "src/module.py")
  - `function_to_modify`: Name of the function to modify (e.g., "ClassName.method_name")
  - `reason`: Technical motivation
  - `side_effects`: Potential impacts

## IMPORTANT RULE for function_to_modify
- If the file to modify is a Python file (.py), specify the function/method to modify
- If the file is NOT a Python file (e.g., .js, .html, .css, .json, .yaml, .md, etc.),
  the value of `function_to_modify` MUST be null

## Guidelines for Steps
- Each step must be atomic and implementable
- The order of steps must be logical (dependencies first)
- For Python files: always specify the function in the format "FunctionName" or "ClassName.method_name"
- For non-Python files: function_to_modify must be null
- The side_effects should mention tests, dependents, and API impacts
- You MUST generate a number of step EQUAL to the number of the python functions you are intendeed to modify nomore neither less

## Step Examples

### For Python files:
```json
{
  "operation": "Add time_spanned_alpha method",
  "file_to_modify": "manimlib/animation/animation.py",
  "function_to_modify": "Animation.time_spanned_alpha",
  "reason": "To compute animation progress based on actual elapsed time",
  "side_effects": "May require downstream animations to call this method"
}
```

### For non-Python files:
```json
{
  "operation": "Update API endpoint configuration",
  "file_to_modify": "config/routes.yaml",
  "function_to_modify": null,
  "reason": "Add new route for the authentication endpoint",
  "side_effects": "Requires server restart to apply changes"
}
```

Generate an implementation plan for this PR:

## PR Title
fix #2331: solve problems in sensevoice_bin.py related to argmax and unique operations.

## PR Body
This PR fixes two issues in sensevoice_bin.py:
- Replace PyTorch argmax usage with NumPy argmax to avoid potential TypeError when dim parameter is not applicable. The change computes the index of the maximum logits per time step using np.argmax(x, axis=-1).
- Replace torch.unique_consecutive-based deduplication with a NumPy-based consecutive-duplicate removal approach. This aligns with torch.unique_consecutive behavior (removes only consecutive duplicates) by constructing a boolean mask that preserves the first element and drops consecutive duplicates, then applying the mask to obtain the deduplicated sequence. Finally, remove any blanks (self.blank_id) and decode tokens as usual.

Rationale:
- np.argmax(x, axis=-1) is the correct NumPy-equivalent for determining per-frame max indices and avoids TypeError from missing dim param.
- torch.unique_consecutive returns only consecutive duplicates, but the previous approach with np.unique removed all duplicates and sorted values, leading to mismatches. The proposed mask-based approach preserves the intended consecutive-duplicate removal behavior and supports proper decoding.

Impact:
- The sensevoice_bin inference path should now robustly handle argmax and consecutive-duplicate removal across CPU/GPU contexts, producing the same (or expected) token sequences as the torch-based approach intended by the original model behavior.

Tests:
- Validate by running inference on a small batch and ensuring decoded text matches expectations for representative inputs.
- Ensure behavior remains consistent for both CPU and GPU if applicable.

## MASCA Analysis (optimized)
MASCA Optimized Analysis focused on PR #2331:

Targeted Change:
- runtime/python/libtorch/funasr_torch/sensevoice_bin.py -> SenseVoiceSmall.__call__
  - Modify per-frame argmax computation from PyTorch tensor-based to NumPy-based (np.argmax with axis) and remove usage of torch.unique_consecutive for deduplication. Implement a consecutive-duplicate removal via a NumPy mask to preserve the intended behavior.

Why this change is needed:
- The prior code used x.argmax(dim=-1) and torch.unique_consecutive, which can lead to type/method mismatches in some environments and does not align with the intended consecutive-duplicate removal semantics described in issue #2331.
- Using np.argmax(x, axis=-1) ensures a NumPy-compatible argmax when needed and avoids potential dim argument issues in certain contexts. The mask-based deduplication duplicates torch.unique_consecutive behavior by removing only consecutive duplicates, preventing unintended global deduplication and reordering.

Risks and mitigations:
- Risk: Data type/device (CPU/GPU) mismatches when converting between PyTorch tensors and NumPy arrays. Mitigation: detach/cpu() before .numpy() to operate on CPU NumPy arrays, then convert back as needed for downstream decoding (which happens on CPU by default in this path).
- Risk: Boundary cases with empty or very short sequences. Mitigation: mask and shape handling via NumPy operations ensures robust handling; token decoding remains gated by existing blank_id masking.

Testing guidance:
- Add/Run inference on a few audio samples to verify that the decoded token sequences match expected outcomes for representative cases (including cases with repeated consecutive logits and varying sequence lengths).
- Verify no regression for CPU/GPU workflows where ctc_logits could be numpy or torch tensor.

Expected outcome:
- Inference now uses a NumPy-based argmax and a consecutive-duplicate removal mask, preserving the intended behavior and improving compatibility across environments.

## Files to Modify
- runtime/python/libtorch/funasr_torch/sensevoice_bin.py: Replace PyTorch argmax usage and torch.unique_consecutive-based deduplication with a NumPy-based approach to compute per-frame max indices and remove consecutive duplicates, ensuring consistent decoding of tokens.

## Functions to Modify
- SenseVoiceSmall.__call__ (runtime/python/libtorch/funasr_torch/sensevoice_bin.py): Modify the per-batch decoding block: compute yseq via NumPy argmax and a mask-based consecutive-duplicate removal instead of x.argmax(dim=-1) followed by torch.unique_consecutive.

## Analysis Summary
Identified target for PR #2331: fix argmax usage and consecutive-duplicate removal in sensevoice_bin.py. The relevant code is in SenseVoiceSmall.__call__ inside runtime/python/libtorch/funasr_torch/sensevoice_bin.py. Proposed changes replace the PyTorch-based argmax and unique_consecutive flow with a NumPy-based approach: (1) compute yseq as np.argmax(x_np, axis=-1) after converting x to a NumPy array; (2) apply a mask to remove only consecutive duplicates using mask = np.concatenate(([True], np.diff(yseq) != 0)); (3) filter out blanks with a second mask and decode. This maintains the intended behavior (consecutive-duplicate removal) and avoids potential issues with torch.unique_consecutive and dim arguments. The plan minimizes risk by operating on CPU-side NumPy arrays, with careful handling of tensor-to-array conversion. The changes are localized to a single function, reducing blast radius and preserving overall architecture.

steps.operation: Refactor per-frame argmax and consecutive-duplicate removal to a NumPy-based implementation
steps.reason: Replace PyTorch-based argmax with NumPy argmax to avoid dim-related TypeError and replace torch.unique_consecutive-based deduplication with a mask-based approach that preserves only consecutive duplicates, aligning with intended behavior described in PR #2331.
steps.side_effects: Introduces NumPy-based code path; may require ensuring NumPy availability and proper handling of tensor-to-NumPy conversions on CPU; downstream decoding may need to handle any subtle changes in token sequences; add/adjust tests for CPU/GPU inference cases

step_plan.summary: This PR fixes argmax handling and consecutive-duplicate removal in sensevoice_bin.py by switching to a NumPy-based approach, ensuring compatibility and correct decoding behavior across environments.
